mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the prior six months signals an ongoing Fed tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as valuation multiples compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose steadily through 2004-2005 tightening cycle
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS uptrends are not significantly negative relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 4.0% and remains there, labor market tightness signals late-cycle demand strength that supports corporate earnings and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during UNRATE<4.0% regimes are not statistically greater than unconditional mean returns, or hit rate falls below 55% over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with declining HOUST jointly signal that higher borrowing costs are suppressing housing activity, an early warning of broader demand deceleration that precedes S&P 500 weakness over the following quarter; neither series alone captures this because rate increases without housing softening reflect resilient demand.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising 3-month trend AND HOUST falling > 5% over 3 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing sensitivity
failure_condition: Joint trigger periods show S&P 500 60-day forward returns not significantly below unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over single-series models (delta Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of decelerating INDPRO growth, industrial production softness reflects weakening supply-side momentum that becomes priced into equities with a short lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month; but during expansion regimes with accelerating INDPRO, the same softening triggers do not translate into losses because it reflects normal cyclical noise rather than a turning point.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns following two consecutive INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or if the effect reverses sign in more than one of three rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS reflects strengthening labor demand that feeds directly into near-term consumer income and equity risk appetite, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month change is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.10) across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above trend signals an inflation surprise that raises expectations of accelerated Fed tightening, causing a rapid repricing of discount rates and a short-horizon negative S&P 500 reaction within one trading week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized change exceeds prior 12-month average by > 1pp
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI surprise triggers are not significantly negative relative to non-trigger periods (p > 0.05), or effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is flat/falling.

mechanism_narrative: Strong RSAFS growth combined with strong PAYEMS growth jointly indicate a self-reinforcing consumption-employment cycle that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; RSAFS growth alone can reflect price-driven nominal spending without genuine employment support, so the interaction is necessary to confirm real demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction effect vanishes once controlling for each series individually (incremental Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a low-growth regime where trailing GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2%, further deceleration signals rising recession risk that depresses equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; in a high-growth regime above 3% GDPC1 growth, the same deceleration is absorbed as normal mean-reversion and does not predict negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 near 19023.82 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: sub-trend growth regime (GDPC1 annualized growth below 2%)
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 deceleration in low-growth regimes are not significantly more negative than in high-growth regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.05), or effect is unstable across recession and non-recession subsamples.
